metadata.title: Distillation: See the Soft Label's Extra Signal

beats.narration_text: Hello. This is Liam, in for Bear. A one-hot label says cat. The teacher says 85 percent cat, 5 percent dog, nothing else — and that 5 percent is the part that trains a better student.

props.command: claude "write distill_demo.py: teacher logits for a 10-class 'cat' image. Compare the one-hot label vs softmax(logits/T) for T=1,3,6. Show mixed loss L = α·hard + (1-α)·soft and how T controls what transfers."
props.segment: Distillation: See the Soft Label's Extra Signal

beats.narration_text: From the textbook: the distillation pipeline. Our CLI script sets up the teacher-student training loop shown here, with soft-label temperature as a tunable parameter.

beats.act: MANIM — One-hot label: cat=1, everything else=0
beats.narration_text: The one-hot label. Cat equals 1. Every other class equals 0. This is what the student gets from the training set. It says cat. It says nothing about the relationship between cat and dog versus cat and airplane. Information is discarded at the label.

The teacher's soft label at temperature 1. Cat: 0.85. Dog: 0.05. The remaining 15 percent is spread across 8 other classes. The teacher is still mostly saying cat — but it is also saying: this image has some dog-like features, just not enough to call it a dog. That 5 percent dog is information the one-hot label erased.

beats.act: MANIM — Temperature sweep: distribution flattens at T=3 and T=6
beats.narration_text: Raise the temperature. At T equals 3, the distribution flattens: cat falls to 0.65, dog rises to 0.12. At T equals 6, even flatter. Higher temperature amplifies the wrong-class relative probabilities — the inter-class similarity signal that the one-hot label throws away. The student trained on this signal consistently outperforms hard-label training by 2 to 5 percent.

Your turn. Ask Claude: write distill_demo.py — teacher logits for a 10-class cat image. Compare the one-hot label versus softmax of logits divided by temperature, for T equals 1, 3, and 6. Show the mixed loss L equals alpha times hard label plus 1 minus alpha times soft label, and how T controls what transfers. Higher temperature amplifies the wrong-class relative probabilities — that is the signal. Add a visualization of what information the one-hot label throws away that the teacher's distribution preserves.

props.command: claude "write distill_demo.py: teacher logits for a 10-class 'cat' image. Compare the one-hot label vs softmax(logits/T) for T=1,3,6. Show mixed loss L = α·hard + (1-α)·soft and how T controls what transfers."

props.title: Distillation: See the Soft Label's Extra Signal
props.subline: embedded ai · liam, in for bear
